sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 62-year-old female, presents with a 3-week history of left shoulder pain.
She reports that the pain is constant and worsens with movement, particularly when reaching or lifting.
She describes the pain as a 7 out of 10 without Tylenol, which reduces the pain to a 2 or 3 with medication.
She denies any direct trauma to the shoulder but has been experiencing aches and pains due to her active lifestyle and recent renovation work.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of occasional aches and pains that are manageable with Tylenol.
She has been renovating her basement due to the pandemic and has been doing a lot of physical activity.
Review of systems: The patient denies numbness or tingling in her arm or hand.
Current medications: I prescribed Tylenol 2 extra strength tablets every 6-8 hours for pain relief.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]

Physical exam: The patient has limited active and passive range of motion in her left shoulder, tenderness of the greater tuberosity of the humerus, and no tenderness at the sternoclavicular or clavicular joints.
Her hand grip is good bilaterally, and neurovascular exam of her left arm shows brisk cap refill in less than 3 seconds and intact motor and sensation to light touch.
Test Results: The patient's X-ray of the left shoulder shows no fracture or bony abnormality.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient's symptoms are most likely due to rotator cuff tendinopathy, indicating injury to the tendons of the muscles of the left shoulder.
Problem list: Rotator cuff tendinopathy; active; suspected.
Plan:
I ordered an MRI of the patient's shoulder to rule out any other conditions.
I referred her for physical therapy for approximately 6-8 weeks to address the rotator cuff tendinopathy.
I instructed her to continue taking Tylenol PRN for pain relief.
If her symptoms do not improve, we may consider a steroid injection to the left shoulder.
Follow-up: I will see the patient in approximately 2 weeks to review her progress and adjust the treatment plan as needed.
